evals.title: Error handling with Catch trigger
evals.prompt: Build a flow that opens a browser, navigates to a URL, and on any error closes the browser cleanly and logs the error.

assertions.reason: Catch is a trigger (Core.Trigger.Catch) — Core.Flow.Catch is a hallucination the SDK validator now hard-rejects.
Log is terminal — must be reached via f.edge() and never chained from.
Catch is a standalone trigger — its outputs must be wired with f.edge(), not .then().

assertions.value: Catch's optNodes targets specific node IDs (no `all: true` unless the flow is meant to catch everywhere); cleanup chain (Close → Stop) is wired with f.edge() from Catch's port 0; Log is a separate leaf off Catch, not in series with Close.

evals.title: ForEach loop over a list
evals.prompt: Build a flow that iterates over a list of items and processes each one in turn.

assertions.reason: Real node ID is Core.Flow.GoTo (capital T). Lowercase variant is rejected by the validator.
GoTo uses optNodes: { ids, type: 'goto', all: false }, NOT inLabel.

assertions.value: Loop body terminates in GoTo back to the Label, ForEach port 1 (done) wires to Stop. No .then() chained after GoTo.

evals.title: Browser scrape with cleanup
evals.prompt: Build a flow that opens Chrome, navigates to a URL, scrapes the page, and closes the browser.

assertions.reason: Enum-valued props are plain literal strings — never wrapped in Custom().
Custom('chrome') wraps an enum literal as a Message scope, which fails the property-type check at runtime.
Core.Browser.ScrapeList / ScrapeTable are forbidden in this skill — use Core.Browser.RunScript returning {columns, rows} JSON.

assertions.value: Close is paired with Open and runs even when scraping returns 0 rows; outBrowserId from Open is reused as inBrowserId on every subsequent browser node.

evals.title: CSV write with headers
evals.prompt: Build a flow that writes a small data table to a CSV file with a header row.

assertions.reason: Schema-only boolean props (optHeaders, optJsonify, etc.) must be emitted explicitly when set to non-default — same family as Designer issue #2378.
Real node is Core.CSV.ReadCSV — Core.CSV.Read does not exist.
Boolean props use literal true/false, never Custom().

assertions.value: Output table shape ({columns, rows}) matches what CSV.WriteCSV expects; csv path is built via global.get('$Home$') in a Function node, not Custom('$Home$/...').

evals.title: Conditional switch
evals.prompt: Build a flow that branches into N different handlers based on a status string using a Core.Programming.Switch node with optConditions, one branch per case wired via f.edge() from each port.

assertions.reason: Core.Programming.If does not exist — use Switch or Function with outputs:2.
Switch port 0 must be wired with f.edge() to the first branch (any 6-hex id).
Switch port 1 must be wired with f.edge() to the second branch (any 6-hex id).

assertions.value: Number of optConditions entries equals the number of f.edge() calls from the Switch node ports; conditions are { scope: 'Custom', name: '<expr>' } objects.

evals.title: Function node with multiple outputs
evals.prompt: Build a flow that uses a Function node to branch into two paths based on a condition.

assertions.reason: Multi-output Function nodes need an explicit numeric outputs:N property.
Function with outputs:2 must return an array — [msg, null] for port 0, [null, msg] for port 1.
Branches must be wired with f.edge() to ports 0 and 1 (any 6-hex id).

assertions.value: func is a literal template string (NOT JS() and NOT Custom()); each branch ends in a non-terminal handler before reaching Stop.

evals.title: Subflow
evals.prompt: Build a flow that delegates work to a subflow defined in a separate file.

assertions.value: Parent main.ts uses Core.Flow.SubFlow node whose ID equals the subflow filename. Subflow file uses subflow.create(name, fn) (not flow.create), Begin → nodes → End({sfPort: 0}). No `subflow:` property on the parent SubFlow node.

evals.title: Credentials via Vault
evals.prompt: Build a flow that fetches an API key from the vault and uses it to authenticate an HTTP request.

assertions.reason: Core.Vault.GetItem REQUIRES optCredentials with the Credential() helper — omitting fails at runtime with 'Vault has to be selected'.
Credential() must appear in the line-1 import line whenever it is used.
No literal API keys / secrets in source — they live in the vault, referenced via vaultId/itemId.

assertions.value: Credential() takes { vaultId, itemId } (UUIDs) — never raw strings or { name, scope } shape. Downstream nodes read individual fields via Message('credentials.<field>').

evals.title: Terminal nodes as leaves
evals.prompt: Build a flow that branches and uses Log, Debug, and Stop as the leaves of each branch.

assertions.reason: Log is terminal — wire it as a leaf via f.edge(), never via .then().
Debug is terminal — wire it as a leaf via f.edge(), never via .then().
GoTo is terminal — chaining .then() after it triggers 'Cannot chain from node (outputs=0)'.

assertions.value: Each terminal (Log, Debug, Stop) is reached only via f.edge(); each is wired from a non-terminal node; no chain extends past any terminal.

evals.title: Required imports complete
evals.prompt: Build a small flow that uses every common SDK helper at least once: fetch a vault item via Core.Vault.GetItem with optCredentials: Credential({vaultId, itemId}), set a Message('header') field in a Function, then make a Core.Net.HttpRequest with a Custom() URL literal. The line-1 import line must include flow, Message, Custom, and Credential — anything referenced in the body but missing from the import line throws ReferenceError at runtime, the #1 cause of robomotion build failures.

assertions.reason: Bun does not warn on unused imports, but a missing helper that is referenced in the body throws 'ReferenceError: <Helper> is not defined' from the bundled flow at runtime — the #1 cause of robomotion build failure.

assertions.value: Import line is on line 1, includes every scope helper used (Message, Custom, JS, Global, Flow, Credential, AI). Even unused helpers should be imported per the canonical convention.

evals.title: Catch-trigger error handling (handle-errors)
evals.prompt: Build a flow that prompts the user for a number > 100 with Core.Dialog.InputBox, validates it in a Core.Programming.Function (throw 'InvalidType' when not numeric, 'InvalidNumber' when ≤ 100), and uses a Core.Trigger.Catch + Core.Programming.Switch on msg.error.message to show the right MessageBox and loop back to the input via Label/GoTo.

assertions.reason: Capital T: GoTo, not Goto.

assertions.value: Catch references monitored node IDs by GUID, switch routes by msg.error.message string, retry path goes Label → InputBox → validation; no .then() after Catch.

evals.title: DuckDuckGo browser scrape (duckduckgo-scraper)
evals.prompt: Build a flow that prompts for a search query, opens Chrome, navigates to duckduckgo.com, types the query, clicks search, waits for results, scrapes Title + Link from each result via Core.Browser.RunScript, writes the rows to an Excel file, and closes the browser.

assertions.reason: ScrapeList / ScrapeTable are forbidden — RunScript returning {columns, rows} JSON instead.

assertions.value: RunScript returns JSON.stringify({columns, rows}); outBrowserId from Open is reused on every subsequent browser node; Close runs even on empty results.

evals.title: Subflow + ForEach over CSV files (merge-csv)
evals.prompt: Build a flow that reads every CSV file under a directory and merges them into one output CSV. Use two subflows (Read CSV Files, Merge CSV) called from a linear main.ts. Configuration (in_dir, csv_path, separator) lives in a Core.Programming.Function 'config' node; the main flow ends in Core.Flow.Stop.

assertions.reason: If does not exist — use Switch or Function with outputs:2.

assertions.value: Each Core.Flow.SubFlow node ID maps to a file under subflows/<id>.ts; subflows use subflow.create(name, fn) with Begin → … → End({sfPort: 0}).

evals.title: Vault credentials in a 3rd-party loop (ssl-watch)
evals.prompt: Build a flow that reads a Google Sheets table of domains using a vault-stored credential, then for each row uses Robomotion.Monitoring.SSL to check expiry and validity and writes the results back into the sheet via SetCellValue. Loop with Label → ForEach → body → GoTo. Declare the GoogleSheets and Monitoring packages with f.addDependency().

assertions.reason: Credential() is required — accept inline object literal or a hoisted const reference.
No literal secrets in source — they live in the vault.

assertions.value: Credential() carries { vaultId, itemId } UUID strings; non-Core packages each get an f.addDependency() with a pinned version; ForEach port 1 wires to Stop.

evals.title: REST API server with HttpIn/HttpOut (rest-api)
evals.prompt: Build a flow that runs a small REST API server on the robot. Expose POST /echo (echo back the request body) and GET /date (return the current date). Both routes share a single Core.Net.HttpOut response node wired via f.edge() from each handler.

assertions.value: Each HttpIn route is its own entry chain; the shared HttpOut node is a single f.node() reached via f.edge() from every handler — never duplicated per route.
